headline: pki.crmf.build issues RFC 4211 certificate request messages -- a CertReqMessages with a signature proof of possession, over every algorithm the toolkit supports.
summary: The CMP/EST enrollment producing side. pki.crmf.build assembles a CertReqMessages (RFC 4211) -- one or more CertReqMsg, each a CertRequest carrying a CertTemplate of the requested certificate fields (subject, the public key being certified, validity, requested extensions) plus a POPOSigningKey proof of possession signed with the private half of the requested key, exactly as a PKCS#10 CSR proves possession. When the template carries both a subject and a public key the signature covers the CertRequest directly; an incomplete template signs a POPOSigningKeyInput instead. The signature algorithm is resolved from the requested public key through the same registry the certificate, CSR, and CMS signers use, so RSA (PKCS#1 v1.5 or PSS), ECDSA, EdDSA, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, and the composite arms all sign without a per-algorithm branch, and the proof of possession is verified before the message is returned. The message carries optional registration controls and regInfo (regToken, authenticator, utf8Pairs, oldCertID, protocolEncrKey, or pre-encoded AttributeTypeAndValue DER), builds a batch from an array of message specs, and drops into a CMP (RFC 9810) or EST enrollment body -- the CA-assigned template fields (serialNumber, signingAlgorithm) and the deprecated unique identifiers are never emitted, and the negative certReqId sentinel (RFC 9483) is honored. Returns DER, or a PEM block with opts.pem; malformed input throws a typed CrmfError. Certificate-request-message parsing remains pki.schema.crmf.parse.

Added: pki.crmf.build(spec, key, opts) builds and DER-encodes an RFC 4211 CertReqMessages -- a CertTemplate of the requested certificate fields plus a POPOSigningKey proof of possession signed with the requester's key (or a raVerified proof, opted into without a key) -- and returns DER, or a PEM block with opts.pem. The signing algorithm is resolved from the requested public key: RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 / PSS, ECDSA, EdDSA, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, or a composite arm. Requested v3 extensions, registration controls, and regInfo ride in the message; pass an array of specs for a batch. Certificate-request-message parsing remains pki.schema.crmf.parse.

pki.asn1.build.implicit(tag, tlv) retags an encoded universal TLV as a context-class IMPLICIT [tag], preserving the source's primitive/constructed bit and content -- the single home for the IMPLICIT tag replacement the CertTemplate and other context-tagged structures compose.
